evidence_snippets: TP-Link FAQ 3062 documents Omada Gateway Emergency Mode with PC static IP 192.168.0.20/24, recovery page at 192.168.0.10, firmware .bin upload, and automatic reboot after upgrade.
collector_notes: Official series-level Web Recovery evidence for listed Omada Gateway models. No profile generation is allowed without model-level evidence and reviewer approval.

evidence_snippets: TP-Link FAQ 3186 documents EAP Recovery Mode with model-dependent static IP rules, HTTP-only recovery page access, official firmware use, and a TFTP path where the PC is the TFTP server and the EAP is the TFTP client.
collector_notes: Official series-level EAP recovery evidence. It can inform Web Recovery and Active TFTP workflow research, but no profile generation is allowed without model-level evidence and reviewer approval.

evidence_snippets: TP-Link FAQ 2954 documents Pharos firmware recovery using TFTP server software on the computer, static IP 192.168.0.100/24, firmware file name recovery.bin, and automatic reboot after transfer.
collector_notes: Official series-level Pharos Active TFTP evidence. No profile generation is allowed without model-level evidence and reviewer approval.

evidence_snippets: ASUS FAQ 1033090 documents Lyra Rescue Mode with static IP 192.168.1.10/24, solid purple Rescue Mode indication, upload through Firmware Restoration, LED changes during upload/reboot, and solid white when Rescue Mode is finished.
collector_notes: Official series-level ASUS Lyra Rescue Mode evidence. No profile generation is allowed without model-level evidence and reviewer approval.
